reasoning: The Lean statement is logically equivalent to the informal claim. "Infinitely many naturals n" is correctly encoded as ∀ N, ∃ n > N. Although the informal text says "integers", any integer that is a sum of two squares is non-negative, so restricting n to ℕ loses nothing. Similarly, since (-a)² = a², a non-negative integer is a sum of two squares of integers iff it is a sum of two squares of naturals, so using ℕ for the witnesses i 0,...,i 5 is also faithful. The three conditions on n, n+1, n+2 are correctly expressed with independent pairs of witnesses via Fin 6 → ℕ.
